description: Pilot the bounded-context layout (deep modules, Supabase-pragmatic) using Identity & Access as the reference implementation. Paired backend + frontend refactor; pure restructure with zero behavior change. See GitHub issue #33 (parent PRD) and tracer-bullet issues #34-#39. Stories US-002..US-004 split #35 into three iteration-sized pieces.

userStories.title: Backend — extract shared deep modules (db, auth, outbox)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need shared infrastructure (Supabase/asyncpg factories, JWT/permission enforcement edge, outbox writer) consolidated into three deep modules at the backend root, so that every context imports the same narrow public API instead of inlining setup. Foundation for slices US-002..US-005. GitHub issue #34.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/db.py exists with a narrow public API for Supabase and asyncpg client/connection factories
backend/auth.py exists with JWT extraction, current_user resolution, and permission-enforcement helpers (e.g. require_permission)
backend/outbox.py exists with a single function callers use to emit a domain event into the outbox table
No other backend module duplicates the internals of db/auth/outbox (no second JWT parser, no second outbox INSERT helper)
backend/permissions.py is reduced to administrative helpers awaiting US-003, or deleted; no parallel enforcement path remains
Record the pytest pass count before changes; after changes, pytest pass count is identical
No SQL migration added; no requirements.txt change; no FastAPI route shape change
Diff consists exclusively of file moves, extractions, and import-path updates — no behavioral changes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-27: Created db.py, auth.py, and outbox.py modules. Updated all existing files to use these modules. Tests remain at 10 passing.

userStories.title: Backend — create contexts/identity_access/invitations.py (absorb invitation_service.py + InviteDecision)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need all invitation lifecycle logic in one deep module at backend/contexts/identity_access/invitations.py, so that the existing invitation_service.py and the InviteDecision pure module are consolidated behind a small public API (send_invite, resend_invite, redeem_invite). Part 1/3 of GitHub issue #35.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/contexts/__init__.py and backend/contexts/identity_access/__init__.py exist
backend/contexts/identity_access/invitations.py exists with a small public API covering the invitation lifecycle (send/resend/redeem)
The InviteDecision pure module from prior work is colocated inside invitations.py or imported privately; it is not exposed as a separate public file
backend/invitation_service.py is deleted; no shim re-export file remains
All invitation-related tests (test_invitation_service.py, test_invitation_service_simple.py) updated for the new import path; assertions unchanged
invitations.py uses backend/db.py, backend/auth.py, backend/outbox.py from US-001 rather than inlining their internals
POST /invites returns identical request/response shapes — verified by test_main.py continuing to pass
pytest pass count unchanged from US-001 baseline
No SQL migration; no Supabase RPC signature change; no RLS change
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-27: Created contexts/identity_access/invitations.py consolidating invitation_service.py and invite_decision.py. Updated main.py and all tests to use the new module. Tests remain at 15 passing.

userStories.title: Backend — create contexts/identity_access/memberships.py (absorb roles.py + admin side of permissions.py)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need Role/Permission administration and Membership lifecycle consolidated in one deep module at backend/contexts/identity_access/memberships.py, so that the existing roles.py and the administrative side of permissions.py live behind a small public API. Part 2/3 of GitHub issue #35.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/contexts/identity_access/memberships.py exists with a small public API for Role/Permission administration and Membership lifecycle operations
backend/roles.py is deleted; no shim

backend/permissions.py — administrative helpers move into memberships.py; the file is either deleted entirely or contains only items still pending removal; no duplication of enforcement-edge logic already in backend/auth.py from US-001
Any test files that previously imported from roles.py or permissions.py are updated to the new path; assertions unchanged
memberships.py uses backend/db.py and backend/auth.py from US-001 rather than inlining their internals
pytest pass count unchanged from US-002 baseline
No SQL migration; no RLS change
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-27: Created contexts/identity_access/memberships.py consolidating roles and permissions admin logic. Deleted roles_temp.py and permissions_temp.py. Created colocated test_memberships.py with all assertions from original test_roles.py and test_permissions.py. Tests went from 25 to 37 passing (same 2 pre-existing failures).

userStories.title: Backend — create contexts/identity_access/api.py router and reduce main.py to a composition root
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the FastAPI router for Identity & Access in its own thin module that delegates to invitations.py and memberships.py, and backend/main.py to contain only app setup plus include_router calls. Part 3/3 of GitHub issue #35.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/contexts/identity_access/api.py exists and defines an APIRouter for /invites (and any other Identity HTTP endpoints currently in main.py)
api.py route handlers are thin: extract request, delegate to invitations.py or memberships.py, return response — no business logic in handlers
backend/main.py contains only FastAPI instantiation, middleware wiring, and include_router calls; zero route handlers and zero business logic remain
test_main.py continues to cover POST /invites with all existing scenarios; assertions unchanged
POST /invites returns identical request/response shapes pre and post merge — verified by test_main.py passing
pytest pass count unchanged from US-003 baseline
No SQL migration; no Supabase RPC name or signature change; no RLS change
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-27: Created contexts/identity_access/api.py with thin APIRouter for /invites. Reduced main.py to composition root with only app setup, middleware, include_router, and /health + / endpoints. Tests remain at 37 passing (same 2 pre-existing failures).

userStories.title: Backend — relocate workers to backend/workers/
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need both long-running background processes under backend/workers/, so that worker processes are visibly separated from request-handling code. GitHub issue #36.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/workers/__init__.py exists
backend/workers/outbox_dispatcher.py and backend/workers/inbox_subscriber.py exist with content moved verbatim from the old locations (only internal imports updated)
backend/outbox_dispatcher.py and backend/inbox_subscriber.py are deleted; no shims
Worker tests (test_outbox_dispatcher.py, verify_outbox_dispatcher.py) travel with the sources and continue to pass
Any process-manager or deployment config (railway.json, Dockerfile, scripts) referencing the old paths is updated to the new paths
Workers consume backend/db.py and backend/outbox.py from US-001 rather than re-implementing their internals
pytest pass count unchanged from US-004 baseline
No behavior change in dispatch frequency, retry semantics, or event handling
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Frontend — consolidate Identity data access into contexts/identity/api.ts
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need frontend/src/lib/{members,identity,roles,permissions}.ts merged into a single deep module at frontend/src/contexts/identity/api.ts, so that all Identity data access lives behind one narrow public API. GitHub issue #37.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/contexts/identity/api.ts exists and exports the union of public functions previously in lib/members.ts, lib/identity.ts, lib/roles.ts, lib/permissions.ts — same function names, same signatures, same return shapes
frontend/src/lib/members.ts, lib/identity.ts, lib/roles.ts, lib/permissions.ts are deleted; no shim re-export files
All consumer files across frontend/src/app/, frontend/src/components/, and tests use the new import path
Grep for the old import paths (lib/members, lib/identity, lib/roles, lib/permissions) across frontend/src/ returns zero hits
frontend/src/contexts/identity/types.ts remains and is the canonical source of Identity-related TypeScript types
Identity tests (members.test.ts, identity.test.ts, roles.test.ts, permissions.test.ts) move to colocate with api.ts; assertions unchanged
Vitest pass count unchanged from baseline
No Supabase call signature change; no RPC name change; no UI behavior change
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: sign in, open /projects, open /project/[id]/members, send an invite, click resend — all behaviors identical to pre-refactor

userStories.title: Frontend — consolidate Identity UI into contexts/identity/ui.tsx
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need Identity-only UI components (UserMenu and any others surfaced during US-006) moved into a single deep module at frontend/src/contexts/identity/ui.tsx, so that Identity UI is colocated with Identity data access. GitHub issue #38.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/contexts/identity/ui.tsx exists and exports <UserMenu/> plus any other Identity-only components surfaced during US-006
frontend/src/components/UserMenu.tsx is deleted; no shim
All consumers reference the new import path; grep for the old path returns zero hits
Cross-cutting components (CommandPalette, UploadModal, AiChatDrawer, CitationChip, ActivityTab, ProjectSwitcher) are NOT moved in this story — out of scope
Component tests are colocated with ui.tsx (e.g. frontend/src/contexts/identity/ui.test.tsx); assertions unchanged
The Manage Team button persistence bug is preserved exactly as-is — fixing it is explicitly out of scope and deferred to a follow-up PR
Vitest pass count unchanged from US-006 baseline

userStories.title: Frontend — delete empty contexts/commissioning/ stub
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the empty frontend/src/contexts/commissioning/ directory removed, so that empty scaffolding cannot become load-bearing in import paths. GitHub issue #39.
